sources.name: OCSTAT — Office cantonal de la statistique de Genève

age_pyramid.source: OCSTAT + BFS STATPOP 2023. NOTE: 5-year brackets by gender NOT available via web search — aggregate percentages only. Full brackets available in OCSTAT table T 01.01.12.03
age_pyramid.data_quality: PARTIAL — aggregates only, 5-year male/female brackets need OCSTAT direct access
age_pyramid.sum_check: M+F = 209,200 — within 1.2% of 2023 BFS pop (206,635). Difference due to rounding of estimated brackets.
age_pyramid.note: 5-year bracket counts are ESTIMATES derived from aggregate percentages and total population. Must be verified against OCSTAT direct data for publication.

top_nationalities.source: QC-corrected: original 36,500 was canton-level. Scaled to commune using pop ratio GE-commune/GE-canton ≈ 0.39, rounded
top_nationalities.note: estimate — commune share of cantonal figure
estimate — intl orgs concentration in commune

nationality.note: QC FIX: Original counts were canton-level (summed to 128,670, exceeding commune foreign pop of 103,800). Scaled to commune-level using GE commune/canton pop ratio (~0.39) with adjustments for commune concentration. Top 10 now sum to ~71,300 (69% of foreign pop). Remaining ~32,500 from 150+ other nationalities — consistent with Geneva's extreme diversity.

languages.source: Swiss Federal Census 2000 — last commune-level language data. Post-2010 structural survey only gives cantonal estimates.
languages.data_quality: OLD — 25+ years. Linguistic landscape has shifted significantly.

employment.source: Ville de Genève — Portrait économique (IREG 2022 report citing BFS STATENT 2019)
employment.note: Geneva commune has more jobs (~215k) than residents (~209k). The difference is filled by frontaliers and domestic commuters.

income.note: Median taxable income NOT found via web search. Available in OCSTAT domain 20.02 (T 20.02 tables). Monthly gross salary is canton-level, not commune-specific.

education.data_quality: OLD — 15 years. Tertiary attainment has likely increased further.

housing.source: BFS housing census 2022 (ownership), OCSTAT / Batimag 2025 (vacancy), RealAdvisor (rents)
housing.note: Extremely tight housing market. Among lowest vacancy rates in Switzerland.

commuters.domestic_pendlers_note: Domestic inbound/outbound commuter flows NOT found via web search. Data exists in BFS Pendlermobilitaet tables.
commuters.frontaliers_source: RTS / SEM STAF 2024 record year — 115k is canton-level. QC-reconciled: context JSON had 115k, this file had 112k. Using 115k (more recent RTS reporting).
commuters.note: Record frontalier numbers in 2024. Canton-level figure — city-specific breakdown not publicly available.

international_orgs.note: 38 international organizations (UN European HQ, CERN, WHO, WTO, ICRC, WIPO, ILO, ITU, etc.) + 750+ NGOs. ~36k total international sector jobs.

institutions.name: Graduate Institute (IHEID)
institutions.note: 93% international — most international institution in corridor

institutions.name: HES-SO Genève (HEG, HEAD, HEdS, HEPIA, HEM, HETS)
institutions.note: 6 schools of applied sciences under HES-SO umbrella

universities.note: Geneva has significant academic weight (~25k students, 12% of population). UNIGE alone is comparable to UNIL. IHEID is uniquely international (93% foreign students).

data_quality_flags: Age pyramid 5-year brackets: ESTIMATED from aggregates — verify with OCSTAT T 01.01.12.03
Language data from 2000 census — severely outdated
Nationality top 5-10: rough estimates from cantonal proportions
Domestic commuter flows: NOT AVAILABLE — need BFS Pendlermobilitaet
Median taxable income: NOT AVAILABLE — need OCSTAT domain 20.02
